title: Pharmacy services assistant
change: Occupational standard and assessment plan revised
overviewOfRole: Supporting the pharmacy team in the delivery of safe and effective pharmacy services.
ssa2: 1.2 Nursing, and subjects and vocations allied to medicine

knowledges.detail: How to maintain the interests of individuals and groups, making patients and their safety the first concern within pharmacy services.
Principles that underpin person-centred care and support.
Principles of equality, diversity and inclusion and cultural differences, and how person-centred care is not compromised because of personal values and beliefs.
Communication techniques to maximise understanding including when individuals have specific communication needs or wishes.
Principles of informed consent as they apply to the role.
Principles that underpin confidentiality of individuals using pharmacy services.
Principles of information governance, in line with GDPR, as required by the role.
The safe use of digital technologies within pharmacy services.
Procedures and systems to follow when recognising and raising concerns, even when it is not easy to do so.
Procedures and systems to follow when recognising and raising concerns about safeguarding, particularly children and vulnerable adults.
Systems and ways of working independently and as part of a team when undertaking pharmacy tasks and assisting in the provision of pharmacy services.
Legal and regulatory requirements that underpin the safe, effective and accurate dispensing and supply of medicines and medical devices, including advice on their use.
The limits of own knowledge and skills and how to seek support and refer to others when needed.
The roles and responsibilities of those they work with and functions of the wider pharmacy and healthcare system.
The importance of team working within the wider pharmacy and healthcare system.
How to refer issues and individuals as appropriate to another member of the pharmacy team, other health and social care staff, organisations or services.
Health and safety policies relevant to the role, recognising hazards and acting appropriately to avoid harm to themselves and others.
Legal and regulatory requirements, local processes and standard operating procedures as applicable to the role.
How to use feedback on performance, local HR processes and reflection, to identify and act on own learning needs.
Principles that underpin ‘best practice’ in the delivery of safe and effective pharmacy services, relevant to the role.
The importance of own and others mental health, wellbeing and resilience.
Sustainable working practices, including the efficient use of resources and how to recycle, reuse and safely dispose of waste in line with organisational policies.

behaviours.detail: Act with integrity and have the courage to say when something goes wrong.
Committed to continuously developing their own professional practice.

skills.detail: Maintain the interests of individuals and groups, making patients and their safety the first concern.
Provide person-centred care and support in pharmacy settings, including settings where patients are not physically present.
Respect equality, diversity and inclusion and cultural differences, ensuring that person-centred care is not compromised because of personal values and beliefs.

Listen to and communicate effectively with users of pharmacy services, which may include individual patients, carers, other members of the pharmacy or healthcare team, other health and social care staff using a range of techniques to determine their needs.
Adapt information and communication style to meet the needs of particular audiences and communication channels.
Apply the principles of consent as appropriate to the role.
Maintain confidentiality of individuals using pharmacy services.
Apply the principles of information governance as required by the role.
Use digital technology in pharmacy services in line with organisation and GPhC policies.
Assist in the provision of pharmacy services as required by the role.
Dispense and supply medicines and medical devices as required by the role.
Advise on the use of medicines and medical devices as required by the role.
Work within the limits of own knowledge and skills, seeking support and referring to others when needed.
Work effectively as part of the pharmacy team and the wider healthcare team.
Refer issues and individuals as appropriate to another member of the pharmacy team, other health and social care staff, organisations or services.
Apply health and safety policies relevant to the role, recognising hazards and acting appropriately to avoid harm to self and others.
Apply and work within the legal and regulatory requirements, local processes and standard operating procedures as applicable to the role.
Use feedback on performance and reflection, to identify and act on own learning needs.
Apply legal and regulatory requirements, including best practice in the context of the role, using systems and accurate performance of pharmacy tasks.
Promote own and others mental health, wellbeing and resilience.
Apply sustainable working practices, including the efficient use of resources and how to recycle, reuse and safely dispose of waste in line with organisational policies.

involvedEmployers: General Pharmaceutical Council (GPhC), Practice Plus Group, South London and Maudsley NHS Trust, Well Pharmacy, Sherwood Forest Hospitals NHS Trust, East Suffolk and North Essex Foundation Trust, Lincolnshire Co-Operative Group, Leeds Teaching Hospitals NHS Trust and NHS England, North East and Yorkshire Workforce, Training and Education Directorate (WT&E), University Hospitals Southampton NHS Foundation Trust, Dudley Group of Hospitals NHS Trust, Cumbria, Northumberland Tyne and Wear NHS Trust, Royal Wolverhampton NHS Trust, Nottingham University Hospitals NHS Trust, NHS England WTE Directorate, Imaan Healthcare, NHS Rotherham, Bradford College, Walsall College, Sheffield College
englishAndMathsQualifications: <p>English and maths qualifications must be completed in line with the <a rel="noopener" href="https://www.gov.uk/guidance/apprenticeship-funding-rules" target="_blank">apprenticeship funding rules</a>.</p>
reviewDetails: this apprenticeship will be reviewed in accordance with our change request policy.
clusterDescription: Order, prepare and dispense medicines under direct supervision

qualifications.title: General Pharmaceutical Council (GPhC) recognised Pharmacy Services Assistant qualification or GPhC accredited Pharmacy Services Assistant programme. Training provider and Assessment Organisation (AO) must be approved by GPhC the regulatory body.
qualifications.anyAdditionalInformation: Please refer to the Awarding Organisation to confirm if English and maths is a regulatory requirement or is an essential component of any mandatory qualification.

duties.dutyDetail: To work under the supervision of a registered pharmacy professional and within the limits of own competence, following the relevant local and national standards, policies and protocols used in the workplace.
Contribute to the daily running and administration of an effective and efficient pharmacy service or team.
Use communication methods and techniques to overcome barriers and meet individuals’ wishes, preferences and needs.
Support the pharmacy team in the safe and effective delivery of pharmacy services.
Record, report and store information related to individuals, applying information and governance.
Maintain own and others’ safety at work.
Maintain and further develop own skills and knowledge.
Support sustainable practice in the delivery of pharmacy services.
